results.possibleIdentity: Duncan E. Morrill was an engineer and technical researcher active in the fields of electronics, data processing, and mathematics from the early 1960s through the 1980s. He received his education at English High School in Boston (class of 1944) and the University of Mississippi (class of 1951). In the early 1960s, he served as a Research Associate at the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution (WHOI), where he was part of the scientific departments and supporting services personnel. During the late 1960s and early 1970s, Morrill was employed by the Lockheed Electronics Company in Plainfield, New Jersey, focusing on technical areas such as computers, electronics, and design. During this period, he resided in Warren, New Jersey, and was a participant in Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization dedicated to fostering collaborations between artists and engineers. By the late 1970s, he was associated with the Interstate Electronics Corporation and was involved in the early developmental history of the Ada programming language, notably participating in the 1978 Irvine Workshop on the DoD Common High Order Language. He is most widely recognized for co-authoring the influential paper 'The Real Ada: Countess of Lovelace' with Carol L. James, published in ACM SIGSOFT Software Engineering Notes in January 1983, which provided historical research on the life and contributions of Ada Lovelace.
